FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Regional M&A heating up as First Hawaiian eyes TriCo; JPMorgan and Goldman ride AI wave; regulatory headwinds emerging on immigration and CFPB staffing.

bullets.text: First Hawaiian Bank acquiring California's TriCo Bancshares for $2B, closing by year-end—marks mainland U.S. re-entry after BNP Paribas exit.
Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan posting record revenues on AI-fueled trading and investment banking, capturing outsized deal flow from the AI boom.
Regulators issue guidance requiring banks to identify and monitor lending risks tied to unauthorized workers—potential portfolio review trigger for lenders with immigrant exposure.
CFPB nominee Brian Johnson wins 60-day review period on staff-cut plan post-confirmation, giving Trump's pick discretion over bureau workforce reduction.

Russell Investments' sale to CalPERS expands existing ties and paves way for Vanguard-like reputational boost -- not to mention a reprieve from debt albatross, if it plays its cards right, analyst says

Hightower's 'Signature' growth soars and recruiter smells 'economics' • Strive CEO says 'never' means 'never, ever'• Ex-Fido CTO nets $7.7 million backing for RIA idea • Mariner vs. client age timebomb • After SpaceX win, Choreo rolls-up 4th RIA • Avantis adds $50B and $30B is net new, whew!
Betterment Faces Class Action Lawsuit Over Cash Sweep Accounts

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: Four stories span regulatory expansion, capacity pressures, and tech consolidation; UK captive regime opens new market while US grid stress and capital diversification reshape insurance fundamentals.

bullets.text: UK regulators propose new captive insurance regime to unlock multi-billion-pound market, signaling openness to alternative insurance structures and potential capital inflows.
PJM grid capacity auction failed to meet supply targets with power costs up 60% due to AI data-center demand—structural supply crunch affecting 65M people across 13 states.
Cedents now prioritizing reinsurers with dual access to traditional and alternative capital; Guy Carpenter's renewal data shows structural shift toward diversified capacity providers.
Duck Creek acquires Send Technology Solutions to build agentic underwriting platform; Coalition launches $25M cyber product for large enterprises following Allianz expansion.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Stripe-Advent eye PayPal at $53B+ while CMBS distress spreads; AI bubble warning amid fintech infrastructure shifts.

bullets.text: Stripe and Advent jointly bid for PayPal at >$53B valuation—a consolidation play that would reshape payments competitive dynamics and fintech M&A precedent.
CMBS special servicing rates jumped in June 2026 driven by large retail, office, and lodging distress, signaling deteriorating credit quality and impending servicer workouts.
BIS flags $1 trillion AI investment boom as vulnerable to sharp correction with systemic spillover risk to global finance.

PE & Deals.lede: PE heavyweights bet big on AI infrastructure while LPs pivot to secondaries; infrastructure plays and niche acquisitions round out the week.

bullets.text: Blackstone, H&F, and Anthropic launch Ode, an AI services provider backed by $5B+ in commitments from Apollo, Goldman Sachs, Sequoia, and others—PE's latest play for AI infrastructure upside.
Louisiana Teachers exploring secondaries sale to trim PE exposure and exit underperforming GPs—a sign LPs are rotating out of legacy buyout positions.
Blackstone, Apollo, KKR deploy $5.34B into Williams energy projects while GIP snaps up majority stake in Summit Ridge Energy—infrastructure remains a core capital sink.
HighVista staffs up its GP-led secondaries team; Stone Point/Kroll acquire ABC Economics; Ardian backs healthcare recruitment—mid-market activity picking up across sectors.

Markets & Macro.lede: PayPal faces a $53bn takeout bid from Stripe and Advent as banks post record earnings but face cyclical headwinds; meanwhile, EU banking deregulation and a major telecom debt crisis add layer complexity.

bullets.text: Stripe and Advent Capital jointly bid $53bn for PayPal; shares up 19% pre-market. Signals PE appetite for large fintech assets despite valuation pressure.
Major U.S. banks reported record Q2 earnings on IB and trading, but geopolitical tension, rate-cut expectations, and CRE exposure create downside risk to the cycle.
EU plans to relax bank capital requirements, mirroring U.S./UK deregulation and potentially reshaping cross-border competitive dynamics.
Altice International faces creditor accusations of defaulting on €2bn of debt through covenant-violating asset transfers.

Newsletters.lede: Wall Street banks post record revenues on trading and M&A strength; Fed signals hawkish inflation stance as CPI cools; structural product innovation accelerates across wealth management.

bullets.text: Record Wall Street haul driven by Iran-related trading volatility and elevated M&A activity, with investment banking units seeing outsized gains.
Fed Governor Warsh signals hard line on inflation to Congress as June CPI lands at 3.5%—above 2% target but down from May's 4.2%.
Vanguard and Envestnet partner on tools to reduce tax friction during portfolio model conversions, addressing key RIA workflow friction point.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: Four stories spanning incentive misalignment, AI infrastructure M&A creativity, and legacy tech constraints—plus a counterintuitive data point on youth employment.

bullets.text: Groq's Jonathan Ross engineered a $20B NVIDIA partnership through unconventional financing (Grok Bonds) after near-bankruptcy—a playbook for AI infrastructure startups navigating capital scarcity.

IBM's earnings miss reflects a deeper problem: its mainframe cash cow (the legacy moat) starves investment in AI competition, trapping the company between defending old franchises and pivoting to new markets.

NBA player behavior (end-of-quarter heaves to pad individual shooting %) exemplifies principal-agent misalignment—a framework applicable to any comp-driven organization where metrics diverge from team outcomes.
Youth unemployment (20-24) has flatlined despite the AI boom, suggesting labor market resilience or that automation fears may be priced in differently than headline narratives suggest.

all.lede: Regional bank M&A heats up with First Hawaiian's $2B TriCo deal, while a potential $53B Stripe-Advent PayPal bid reshapes fintech consolidation.

bullets.text: First Hawaiian Bank acquiring California's TriCo Bancshares for $2B, closing by year-end—marks Hawaiian bank's return to mainland U.S. after BNP Paribas exit.
Stripe and Advent Capital jointly bid $53B+ for PayPal; shares surge 19% pre-market on deal signal. Private equity moving aggressively into large fintech consolidation.
PayPal buyout report confirms PE appetite for large fintech assets despite valuation pressure—deal would pair two of fintech's largest players.
